title: [Bug]: RetryPolicy direct construction accepts negative max_retries
summary: The public RetryPolicy dataclass can be constructed directly with a negative retry count even though configuration validation rejects it.
observed: Direct Python API use can create a retry policy with a negative max_retries value.
expected: RetryPolicy must validate its own invariants so direct API construction is as safe as configuration-driven construction.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that constructs RetryPolicy with a negative max_retries value.
impact: Embedding applications can create inconsistent retry behavior if they bypass the JSON configuration model.
delivery_semantics: The fix keeps retry policy bounded and explicit without changing ACK ordering.
security: Validated public APIs reduce unsafe configuration drift in embedded deployments.
acceptance: RetryPolicy rejects negative max_retries values during construction.
Configured retry behavior remains unchanged for valid values.
Existing retry tests continue to pass.
The focused regression test and full checks pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, retry tests, commit-then-ack tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update retry documentation or release notes if public RetryPolicy validation is clarified.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published with regression and verification evidence.
